title: senecio adnivalis var. stanleyi
intro: Senecio adnivalis var. stanleyi, also known as the Woolly Ragwort, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its pyrrolizidine alkaloids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions, although its use is cautioned due to potential liver toxicity. In herbal preparations, Senecio adnivalis var. stanleyi is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
